---
title: Error codes
description: CLI exit codes and structured error codes raised by SentinelQA.
status: Stable
---

<!-- DO NOT EDIT — this file is generated by scripts/docs/gen_error_codes.py. Regenerate with `make docs-gen-error-codes`. -->

SentinelQA's CLI exits with a fixed code grid (PRD §13.2 / CLAUDE.md §13). Structured error codes are emitted via the agent-message contract and surfaced in `audit.log`.

## CLI exit codes

| Code | Name | Meaning |
|---:|---|---|
| 0 | Success | The audit completed without quality-gate failures. |
| 1 | Quality gate failed | Findings or score crossed a configured policy threshold. |
| 2 | Invalid config | `sentinel.config.yaml` failed schema validation, or the CLI was invoked with incompatible options. |
| 3 | Runtime error | An unexpected runtime error occurred (network unreachable, disk full, etc.). |
| 4 | Unsafe target | The resolved target was not local and not on the allow-list, or a destructive mode was requested without a valid proof-of-authorization. |
| 5 | Dependency missing | A required binary (Node, pnpm, Playwright, `sentinel-ts`) was not found. |
| 6 | Test execution failed | The runner crashed or could not complete the planned tests. |
| 7 | Internal error | Bug in SentinelQA itself; file an issue. |

## Structured error codes

Each code below maps to one of the CLI exit codes above. The message template uses Python `str.format` placeholders; the actual values are interpolated at raise time.

## Exit 1 — Quality gate failed

| Code | Message template | Suggested fix |
|---|---|---|
| `E-QGATE-001` | Quality gate failed: {detail} | Either fix the underlying findings or adjust `policy` in `sentinel.config.yaml` if the gate is genuinely too strict. |

## Exit 2 — Invalid config

| Code | Message template | Suggested fix |
|---|---|---|
| `E-CFG-001` | Configuration file is missing or unreadable: {path} | Create `sentinel.config.yaml` at the project root or pass `--config <path>` pointing at an existing file. |
| `E-CFG-002` | Configuration failed schema validation: {detail} | Run `sentinel doctor` for a precise diff against the expected schema (PRD §17.1). |
| `E-CFG-003` | Inline secret detected at config key {field!r}; secrets must come from environment variables. | Replace the literal value with the corresponding `*_env` key (e.g. `password_env: TEST_USER_PASSWORD`). |

## Exit 4 — Unsafe target

| Code | Message template | Suggested fix |
|---|---|---|
| `E-SAFE-001` | Host {host!r} is not in target.allowed_hosts and is not local. | Add the host to `target.allowed_hosts` only if you own or are authorized to test it. SentinelQA never permits unauthorized scans (PRD §2, CLAUDE.md §6). |
| `E-SAFE-002` | Destructive mode requested for {host!r} without a valid proof-of-authorization document. | Provide `target.proof_of_authorization` pointing at a signed doc that covers this host, actor, and scope, and is not expired. |
| `E-SAFE-003` | Forbidden CLI flag {flag!r} requested; stealth/evasion/bypass features are not part of SentinelQA. | Remove the flag. See PRD §2.1 and CLAUDE.md §6 for the full list of forbidden capabilities. |

## Exit 5 — Dependency missing

| Code | Message template | Suggested fix |
|---|---|---|
| `E-DEP-001` | Required dependency is missing: {dependency} | Run `make install` (or `uv sync --frozen --all-packages` and `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`) and retry. |
| `E-PLG-001` | Plugin {plugin!r} could not be loaded: {detail} | Verify the plugin is installed, declares the expected entry point, and matches the host SentinelQA version. |

## Exit 6 — Test execution failed

| Code | Message template | Suggested fix |
|---|---|---|
| `E-RUN-001` | Test execution failed: {detail} | Inspect the Playwright trace and stdout under `.sentinel/runs/<run-id>/` for the failing step. |

## Exit 7 — Internal error

| Code | Message template | Suggested fix |
|---|---|---|
| `E-INT-001` | Internal SentinelQA error: {detail} | Re-run with `--verbose` and file a bug report including the captured stack trace; secrets are redacted by default. |
| `E-PLG-002` | Plugin {plugin!r} crashed at runtime: {detail} | Disable the plugin via `modules.<name>: false` while you diagnose; SentinelQA core continues without it. |
